response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship credentials (TDD, testing tooling, community teaching) and some high-throughput systems exposure, but they fall well short of the role's core minimum qualifications. C++ experience appears limited to one early-career role and a thesis project — nowhere near 8 years — and their infrastructure background is centered on D-language services, web platforms, and product engineering rather than building large-scale cloud compute platforms. Mentoring exists via workshops and dojos, but there is no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud/DevOps teams or ownership of platform-level technical strategy.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored testing frameworks
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput microservices (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ — only one early role and a thesis project involve C/C++
No formal technical-leadership experience leading cloud platform or DevOps teams
Background is product/full-stack and niche D-language work, not cloud compute platform architecture
